page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It does not support hyperthreading, providing exactly one thread per physical core. The processor configuration includes a 64 KB L1 data cache, a 2 MB L2 cache, and a 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

For local storage, the r8gd.12xlarge includes 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. This instance does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory operations and database workloads. The instance achieves top-tier rankings in large-block memory bandwidth for both read and write operations, as well as in the PassMark memory score and database operations. Multi-core CPU performance is also strong, with Geekbench multi-core and software compilation benchmarks placing in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average category.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the r8gd.12xlarge is driven by its high memory density and the power-efficient ARMv9 architecture, which reduces resource overhead compared to traditional x86 architectures. The combination of high memory capacity, fast local NVMe storage, and top-tier database operation performance makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, software compilation, and static web serving.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8gd.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core software compilation, though single-core CPU performance remains in the average tier. The high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage make this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching, and static web serving.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, massive memory bandwidth, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
